case_title: RAY POON-PHANG SEET MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
DECLARATION OF DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL BRENDA P. REYES IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR STAY FILED BY RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER RAY SEET'S PETITION FOR ADMINISTRATIVE WRIT IS DENIED. IN LIGHT OF ALL THE CIRCUMSTANCES AND THE RECORD BEFORE THE BOARD, PETITIONER FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT RESPONDENT ABUSED ITS DISCRETION IN DENYING THE MOTIONS TO CONTINUE THE ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING. PETITIONER WAS AWARE OF PRIOR COUNSEL'S ILLNESS AND DID NOT MOVE FOR A CONTINUANCE IN A TIMELY FASHION. PETITIONER'S REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE HEARING DID NOT COMPLY WITH GOV'T CODE 11524(B) AND WAS MADE A MERE MONTH BEFORE THE ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING. PETITIONER DECLINED RESPONDENT'S OFFER OF A CONTINUANCE CONDITIONED ON PETITIONER SUSPENDING HIS PRACTICE OF MEDICINE PENDING THE CONTINUED HEARING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. ALL ADMINISTRATIVE RECORDS LODGED WITH THE COURT FOR TODAY'S HEARING ARE RETURNED TO THE SUBMITTING PARTIES. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Administrative Writ
Off Calendar Nov-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOHN FLEER, ESQ., 925.930.9518, FOR PETITIONER RAY POON-PHANG SEET MD.
LYNNE DOMBROWSKI, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 415.709.5578, FOR RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER RAY SEET'S Petition For Administrative Writ is denied. In light of all the circumstances and the record before the Board, Petitioner fails to establish that respondent abused its discretion in denying the motions to continue the administrative hearing. Petitioner was aware of prior counsel's illness and did not move for a continuance in a timely fashion. Petitioner's request to continue the hearing did not comply with Gov't Code 11524(b) and was made a mere month before the administrative hearing. Petitioner declined Respondent's offer of a continuance conditioned on Petitioner suspending his practice of medicine pending the continued hearing. Order signed in open court. Judgment signed in open court. ALL ADMINISTRATIVE RECORDS LODGED WITH THE COURT FOR TODAY'S HEARING ARE RETURNED TO THE SUBMITTING PARTIES. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
